name: Suwannee River
description_short: Suwannee River State Park is a Florida Department of Environmental Protection recreational facility offering camping, cabins, and outdoor activities along the Suwannee River.
description_long: Suwannee River State Park is a state-operated recreational facility managed by the Florida Department of Environmental Protection, located in Live Oak, Florida at the intersection of the Suwannee and Withlacoochee rivers. The park has been operating as a public recreation area for decades and emphasizes historical significance alongside natural conservation. The facility offers overnight accommodations including five riverside cabins and campground facilities (currently undergoing upgrades as of June 2025), along with day-use recreational activities. The park charges a $5 per vehicle entry fee and operates 365 days a year from 8 a.m. until sunset. The park provides a range of outdoor experiences and amenities including camping, cabin rentals, paddling (canoe and kayak launches), fishing, hiking, bicycling, birding, boating, and picnicking. Amenities include boat ramps, RV facilities, laundry, restrooms, picnic pavilions, playgrounds, and interpretive exhibits focused on Civil War history and natural flora and fauna. The park offers canoe and kayak rentals through nearby outfitters and maintains multiple trails for visitors to explore the surrounding wilderness and historical sites. Suwannee River State Park distinguishes itself through its emphasis on historical preservation, particularly relating to Civil War-era sites including remnants of a 19th-century steamship and two ghost towns. The park is recognized by the Green Lodging program and operates as part of Florida's state parks system. It serves as a conservation area for native species and offers a Suwannee River Wilderness Trail for extended exploration of the 246-mile river system. However, this is fundamentally a state park and recreation facility, not a consumer finance company. It provides no financial services, lending products, credit-related services, or consumer finance offerings of any kind. Listing it in any finance category is a significant miscategorization.
services: Camping (currently closed for upgrades as of June 2025)
Riverside cabin rentals with 5 available units
Park maps and recreational planning resources
pros: Open 365 days a year with consistent 8 a.m. to sunset hours for accessibility
Low entry fee of $5 per vehicle makes recreational access affordable
Five riverside cabins provide overnight accommodation options in a natural setting
Multiple outdoor activities available including paddling, fishing, hiking, and boating
Green Lodging certified, indicating environmental sustainability standards
Interpretive exhibits and trails offer educational historical and natural content
Nearby outfitter services available for canoe and kayak rentals
cons: Campground closed to all reservations and walk-ins as of June 1, 2025 due to upgrade project with no reopening date specified
Bridge and River Trail temporarily closed as of November 25, 2025
Overlook remains closed for repairs as of January 1, 2025, limiting scenic viewing access
No financial services or lending products available despite consumer finance category listing
best_for: Outdoor recreation enthusiasts seeking nature-based camping and cabin experiences
Visitors interested in Civil War history and historical preservation sites
Paddlers and boaters exploring the Suwannee River system
Families looking for affordable day-use recreation and picnicking
diagnosis: This is a state park operated by Florida's Department of Environmental Protection and provides exclusively recreational and historical site access—no consumer finance services whatsoever. It should not be listed in any finance category and requires complete recategorization or removal from a consumer finance database.
